tool.name: MAGs-visualization pathway-module-heatmap
tool.description: Create a MAG vs pathway-module heatmap
tool.command:
mags-visualization pathway-module-heatmap --pathways '$pathways' --mode '$mode' --row_fontsize '$row_fontsize' --format '$format' --module_label '$module_label'
#if $module_selection.selection_mode == "top" --top_modules "$module_selection.top_modules" #else --top_modules None #end if
#if $representatives.drep --drep '$representatives.drep' #end if
#if $representatives.gtdb --gtdb '$representatives.gtdb' #end if
#if str($representatives.top_representatives) != "" --top_representatives '$representatives.top_representatives' #end if
@FIG_SIZE_COMMAND@
-o outputs && mv outputs/pathway_module_heatmap_*.${format} outputs/final_plot.png

param.label: Pathway completeness table
param.help: Use a pathway/module completeness table, for example kegg_pathway_completeness.tsv, containing 'contig', 'module_accession', 'completeness', 'pathway_name', and 'pathway_class'. Completeness values are used for the functional module annotation.

param.name: selection_mode
param.label: Number of modules to display
param.help: Choose whether to show only the top-ranked functional modules or all modules from the pathway completeness table.

param.label: Number of top modules
param.help: Number of KEGG modules to display after ranking by the selected mode: highest mean completeness for core modules, or highest variance for differential modules.

param.label: Module ranking mode
param.help: Select how modules are ranked before plotting: 'mean' highlights core modules with high average completeness across MAGs, while 'variance' highlights modules that differ most between MAGs.

param.label: Module label style
param.help: Choose how KEGG modules are labeled in the heatmap: module accession only, module name only, or both accession and name.

param.label: Row label font size (MAG labels)
param.help: Font size for MAG labels on the heatmap rows. Increase this value for readability when plotting fewer MAGs; decrease it when many MAGs are displayed.

section.name: representatives
section.title: Representative MAG selection (optional)
section.help: Restrict the heatmap to representative MAGs from dRep clusters, matching the row order and selection of the drep-cluster-func plot for direct comparison.

param.name: drep
param.label: dRep cluster table
param.help: Provides the cluster assignments (which MAG belongs to which dRep cluster). Required to identify representatives.

param.name: gtdb
param.label: GTDB annotation table
param.help: Used together with the cluster table to pick exactly the same representative per cluster as the drep-cluster-func plot, so both heatmaps show identical MAGs in the same order.

param.name: top_representatives
param.label: Top representative MAGs
param.help: Number of largest dRep clusters to include in the heatmap. If left empty, all MAGs from the pathway table are shown alphabetically. Requires both dRep and GTDB tables.

tool.help:
**MAGs-visualization: pathway-module-heatmap**
This tool generates a heatmap of KEGG pathway module completeness across MAGs. Rows represent MAGs and columns represent KEGG modules. Heatmap values show module completeness percentages, enabling comparison of functional profiles between MAGs.
Modules can be selected using two modes:
- **mean**: highlights core modules shared across MAGs - **variance**: highlights modules that vary most between MAGs
The module selection option allows either displaying the top-ranked modules or all available modules.
**Representative MAG selection - optional:**
By default, all MAGs from the pathway table are shown in alphabetical order.
To restrict the heatmap to representative MAGs from dRep clusters, provide:
- **dRep cluster table** - defines the clusters and their members - **GTDB annotation table** - ensures the same representative genome is selected per cluster as in drep-cluster-func - **Top representative MAGs** - number of largest clusters to include
**Input files**
- **KEGG pathway completeness table** (``--pathways``): use a pathway/module completeness table, for example ``kegg_pathway_completeness.tsv``. The table must contain ``contig``, ``module_accession``, ``completeness``, ``pathway_name``, and ``pathway_class``. The tool uses ``contig`` as the MAG/genome identifier, ``module_accession`` and ``pathway_name`` for module labels, ``pathway_class`` for grouping, and ``completeness`` as the heatmap value.
Example::
contig module_accession completeness pathway_name pathway_class matching_ko missing_ko SRR24759597_bin_27614.fasta M00001 100.0 Glycolysis (Embden-Meyerhof pathway), glucose => pyruvate Pathway modules; Carbohydrate metabolism; Central carbohydrate metabolism K00134,K00845,K00850,K00873,K00927,K01624,K01689,K01803,K01810,K01834 SRR24759597_bin_27614.fasta M00002 100.0 Glycolysis, core module involving three-carbon compounds Pathway modules; Carbohydrate metabolism; Central carbohydrate metabolism K00134,K00873,K00927,K01689,K01803,K01834
- **dRep cluster table** (optional, ``--drep``): use the unmodified dRep cluster table, typically the ``Cdb.csv`` output. It is only needed when representative MAG selection is enabled. The tool uses ``genome`` and ``secondary_cluster`` to identify which MAGs belong to each dRep species-level cluster; additional dRep columns can remain in the file.
Example::
genome,secondary_cluster,threshold,cluster_method,comparison_algorithm,primary_cluster SRR24759596_bin_37640.fasta,1_1,0.050000000000000044,average,ANImf,1 SRR24759600_bin_174663.fasta,1_1,0.050000000000000044,average,ANImf,1 SRR24759601_bin_46010.fasta,1_1,0.050000000000000044,average,ANImf,1
- **GTDB-Tk annotation table** (optional, ``--gtdb``): use an unmodified GTDB-Tk summary/annotation table containing ``user_genome`` and ``classification``. It is used together with the dRep table to select the same representative genome per cluster as in drep-cluster-func. The example below shows the relevant columns only; the original GTDB-Tk output may contain additional columns and can be used unmodified.
Example with the relevant columns shown::
user_genome classification SRR24759597_bin_73064.fasta d__Bacteria;p__Pseudomonadota;c__Gammaproteobacteria;o__Enterobacterales;f__Enterobacteriaceae;g__Frischella;s__Frischella perrara SRR24759598_bin_119213.fasta d__Bacteria;p__Pseudomonadota;c__Gammaproteobacteria;o__Enterobacterales;f__Enterobacteriaceae;g__Gilliamella;s__Gilliamella sp945271295
**Parameters**
- **Number of modules to display**: choose whether to show top-ranked modules or all modules from the pathway completeness table. - **Number of top modules**: number of modules shown when top-module selection is enabled. - **Module ranking mode**: ``mean`` ranks modules by average completeness across MAGs; ``variance`` ranks modules by variability between MAGs. - **Module label style**: controls whether KEGG modules are labeled by accession, name, or both. - **Row label font size**: font size for MAG labels on heatmap rows. - **Representative MAG selection**: optionally restricts the heatmap to representative MAGs from the largest dRep clusters for comparison with drep-cluster-func.
**Output**
- Heatmap of functional profiles across MAGs
**Layout options:** All size and spacing parameters (figure width, height, spacers) are specified in **cm**.
Example output plots are available in the MAGs-visualization use cases with example configurations and parameter choices: https://github.com/usegalaxy-eu/MAGs-visualization/tree/main/use-cases
For a direct example (bee microbiome dataset): https://github.com/usegalaxy-eu/MAGs-visualization/tree/main/use-cases/bee-use-case/plots
